title: Aconitella teheranica
intro: Aconitella teheranica is a perennial plant species in the Ranunculaceae family, commonly found in mountainous regions of the Middle East and Central Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its purported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Aconitella teheranica include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Aconitella teheranica is often processed into tinctures, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into ointments and creams, although its use requires caution due to potential toxicity.
